name: writing-to-logseq
description: Expert in writing data to Logseq DB graphs via HTTP API. Auto-invokes when users want to create pages, add blocks, update content, set properties, or sync conversation notes to their Logseq graph. Provides CRUD operations with safety guidelines.
capabilities: Create pages with initial content and properties
Add blocks as children or siblings of existing content
Update and delete blocks and pages
Set typed properties (text, number, date, checkbox)
Add and remove tags from blocks
author: C0ntr0lledCha0s
securitySummary: Legitimate Logseq integration skill for knowledge management. Static analysis flagged 258 patterns but 99% are false positives from markdown documentation being misidentified as code execution. The actual implementation only makes local HTTP API calls to localhost:12315 with user-configured tokens. No external network access, no shell commands, no credential exfiltration.
valueStatement: Manually copying information to Logseq takes time. This skill provides programmatic access to create pages, add blocks, set properties, and sync conversation notes directly to your Logseq graph via the HTTP API.
limitations: Requires Logseq HTTP API to be enabled with API token
Only connects to local Logseq instance (localhost)
Cannot query or read data (use separate reading skill)
Rate limited by Logseq API responsiveness

useCases.title: Capture AI conversation insights
useCases.description: Automatically sync Claude Code conversations to your Logseq knowledge base with timestamps for later reference.

useCases.title: Create structured project pages
useCases.description: Generate project pages with tasks, properties, and nested blocks following consistent templates.

useCases.title: Build interconnected knowledge graphs
useCases.description: Create linked pages, add tags, and build relationships between research notes and sources.

promptTemplates.title: Create simple page
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Logseq page called '{{Page Title}}' with this content: {{content}}

promptTemplates.title: Add task with properties
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a task block to my '{{Project}}' page with content '{{task description}}', priority '{{priority}}', and due date {{date}}

promptTemplates.title: Sync conversation
promptTemplates.prompt: Sync these conversation notes to Logseq under 'Claude Notes/{{topic}}': {{notes}}

promptTemplates.title: Build knowledge structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a structured Logseq page for '{{subject}}' in the '{{category}}' namespace with overview, key points, and action items sections

bestPractices: Use get_or_create_page() instead of create_page() to avoid duplicate errors
Verify resources exist before deleting with confirm-before-delete checks
Add rate limiting (0.1s delay) between batch operations to avoid overwhelming the API
antiPatterns: Don't use create_page() multiple times with the same title (causes DuplicateError)
Avoid bulk delete operations without size limits (prevents accidental data loss)
Do not modify system properties like uuid, created-at, or block/parent directly

faq.question: Is Logseq API required?
faq.answer: Yes, Logseq must have HTTP API enabled in settings with an API token configured.

faq.question: Can this read Logseq data?
faq.answer: No, this skill only handles write operations. Use the 'reading-logseq' skill for data retrieval.

faq.question: What data types are supported?
faq.answer: Strings, numbers, dates (linked format), checkboxes, and tags are fully supported.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes, all operations target your local Logseq instance only. No data leaves your machine.

faq.question: Why do I get DuplicateError?
faq.answer: The page already exists. Use get_or_create_page() for idempotent operations.

faq.question: How is this different from other skills?
faq.answer: This provides direct HTTP API access for programmatic control. Other methods may use file-based syncing.
